    "summary": "Port is hardcoded to 443 with no override path, so callers that previously connected to non-standard ports (development servers, internal services, non-TLS endpoints) will silently attempt port 443 instead of failing visibly, causing wrong-host connections rather than immediate errors.",

    "summary": "The `Client | null` return type is a false contract: the implementation unconditionally returns `new Client(...)` and never produces null, so the type annotation forces all callers to write null-guards for an unreachable code path while providing no actual safety.",
    "fix": "Either restore the `Client` return type or add the missing null-producing code path (e.g., validation that returns null on bad input) so the declared type matches the real behaviour."
